text: October, nineteen ninety-seven.
In four trading days, the Hang Seng Index loses twenty-three percent of its value.

props.label: Hang Seng Index: down 23% in four trading days

text: Overnight interest rates jump from eight percent to twenty-three. At one point, two hundred and eighty.
And while everyone else is panicking, somebody on that trading floor is making money. Hi. I'm Jordan Bull.

props.title: Jordan Bull
props.subtitle: Your broker for the next twelve minutes

text: I'm going to show you the only product that turns panic into a paycheck. Options.
This is the Hang Seng Index Option. Listed on the Hong Kong exchange since March, nineteen ninety-three. The flagship index derivative of Asia.
Every point of the index is worth fifty Hong Kong dollars. European exercise. Cash settled. No shares change hands, just money.

items.title: Cash settled
items.desc: no shares change hands, just money

text: But I'm not going to lecture you. I'm going to let you live it. We built a trading desk you can play.

visual.description: Game title screen: Central Trader

text: Real accounts. Real progress tracking. Four days from rookie to closer.

visual.description: Login, then dashboard with account and progress

text: Day one starts now. Let's sell something.

visual.description: Dashboard with four day cards, zoom toward Day 1

text: Meet Martin. Twenty years on the desk. His first rule: read the client before you touch the product.

visual.description: Day 1 lesson, mentor Martin card

text: Two basic moves. A call is the right to buy at a fixed price. A put is the right to sell.

visual.description: Call and put lesson cards

text: Neither right is free. You pay a premium. Think of a movie ticket. Buy it, you're in. Movie's terrible? No refunds.

visual.description: Premium lesson, movie ticket line highlighted

text: Here's the call's payoff. Flat, flat, flat, then up. Below the strike you lose only the ticket price. Above it, you participate.

visual.description: Call payoff diagram, hockey stick

text: The put is the mirror. It pays when the market falls. Insurance, basically.

visual.description: Put payoff diagram, mirror of the call

text: First client. Mister Wang. He's bullish on the index, but he cannot stomach a big loss.

visual.description: Client card: Mr. Wang, bullish, low loss tolerance

text: Four shelves. Vanilla call. Vanilla put. Buy the index outright. And a locked barrier product, we'll earn that one later.

visual.description: Product selection desk, four cards, barrier locked

text: Bullish view plus capped downside equals vanilla call. Click.

visual.description: Select vanilla call, Selected badge appears

text: Before he signs, disclosure. If the option expires worthless, the entire premium is gone. We say it out loud. Compliance is not decoration.

visual.description: Risk disclosure checklist, tick the items

text: Now the market runs. Twenty-one five. Twenty-two one. Twenty-two four at expiry. The call lands in the money.

visual.description: Day 1 market run, path ticks up to 22,400

text: But look at the net line. Payoff minus premium. Direction right is not enough, you have to out-earn the ticket price. Remember that number.

visual.description: Report card, client P&L, net line highlighted

text: And in the real contract, every point is fifty Hong Kong dollars. Four hundred points of payoff is twenty thousand dollars. Per contract.

props.label: 400 points of payoff x HK$50 per point, per contract

text: Day two. Mister Wang is back, and today he wants a price. Not a feeling. A number.

visual.description: Day 2 intro, pricing desk

text: Here's the secret nobody tells retail: an option price is not guessed. It is built. Branch by branch.
Start at today. Index at twenty-one thousand five hundred.

visual.description: Tree lesson, today node at 21,500

text: One step ahead, two futures. Up two point six five percent, or down two point five eight. That spread comes from volatility, sixteen percent a year.

visual.description: Up and down nodes appear from the root

text: The up factor is e to the sigma root delta-t. The down factor is just one over it.
And the chance of going up, the risk-neutral probability, is e to the r delta-t minus d, over u minus d. That's the whole machine.
Three steps to expiry. Eight paths. Four final prices, from nineteen nine to twenty-three two.

visual.description: Full three-step tree, eight paths, four final prices

text: At expiry the payoff is simple. Final price minus strike of twenty-two thousand, or zero. Whichever is bigger.

visual.description: Expiry payoffs highlighted on the final nodes

text: Then we walk backwards. Each node is the discounted, probability-weighted average of its two children. Fold the future into the present.

visual.description: Backward induction walk from leaves to root

text: And the tree hands us the answer. One hundred eighty-six points. That is the theoretical price of this call.

visual.description: Root node shows 186, zoom punch

text: Now the sales part. Theory says one eighty-six. The desk needs margin. Quote between plus four and plus thirty-four over theoretical and the client fills.

visual.description: Quote slider with fair band, plus 4 to plus 34

text: Quote fat and he walks to the bank across the street. Quote thin and you ate the risk for free.

visual.description: Quote set too high, client pushes back

text: Two hundred ten. Filled. That spread between one eighty-six and two ten? That's the desk's lunch.

visual.description: Confirm quote at 210, fill confirmation

text: Market drifts to twenty-two one. Small payoff, smaller than the premium. The client learns. So do you.

visual.description: Day 2 market run to 22,140 and report

text: Day three. Miz Chen. Sharp. She wants the same upside protection, but she calls our vanilla price expensive.

visual.description: Day 3 client card: Ms. Chen

text: So we go off the shelf. Over the counter. Welcome to structured products, Hong Kong's favorite aisle.
This is a knock-out barrier option. Same call, same strike, one extra clause. If the index ever touches twenty-one thousand, the contract dies. Instantly.

props.title: Knock-Out Barrier Option
props.subtitle: Same call, same strike, one extra clause: touch 21,000 and it dies

text: Price the vanilla on a four-step tree: one thousand one hundred twelve points. Price the barrier: nine hundred thirty-four.
Same view, one hundred seventy-eight points cheaper. Why? Because she sold back the crash scenarios. The paths that touch the barrier pay nothing, so she doesn't pay for them.

props.label: points cheaper: the crash scenarios are sold back

text: Our quote band scales the same way. Fair up to about one point one eight times theoretical. Reject above one point four.

visual.description: Quote band relative: fair to about 1.18x, reject above 1.4x

text: Now watch the market. Up to twenty-one eight. Then down. Twenty-one four. Twenty thousand nine fifty.

visual.description: Market run starts, dips to 21,460 then 20,950

text: Touch. Twenty-one thousand breached. Knocked out. Position terminated.

visual.description: KNOCKED OUT banner at the 20,950 fixing

text: And then, of course, the market rallies to twenty-two four. She watches the move she predicted happen, with no position.

visual.description: Path recovers to 22,400, position already gone

text: That is the barrier trade-off in one chart. The discount is real, and so is the tripwire. A good desk sells both sentences.

props.text: The discount is real. So is the tripwire.

text: In our model the knock-out checks at discrete fixings. In the real OTC market, the term sheet defines exactly when the barrier observes. Read it.

items.title: Real OTC
items.desc: the term sheet defines when the barrier observes

text: Day four. Martin steps back. Three clients, live quotes, no hints.

visual.description: Day 4 briefing, Martin steps back

text: Mister Zhang. Vanilla call, index at twenty-four thousand, strike twenty-four five, vol eighteen. The tree says two ninety-seven.

visual.description: Client card: Mr. Zhang, vanilla, tree shows 297

text: Quote inside the band. Filled.

visual.description: Quote inside the band, fill confirmation

text: Miz Li. Barrier at twenty-three thousand, vol twenty-eight. Nine eighty-one.

visual.description: Client card: Ms. Li, barrier 23,000, price 981

text: Mister He. Deeper barrier, vol thirty-two. Eleven eighty-four. Three products, three risk profiles, one machine.

visual.description: Client card: Mr. He, deeper barrier, price 1,184

text: The desk grades every quote. Suitability, pricing, disclosure. That's the whole job in three columns.

visual.description: Scorecard, grade A: suitability, pricing, disclosure

text: And everything you just watched is saved to your account. Four days, tracked.

visual.description: Progress dashboard, cloud sync indicator

text: So here's my pitch. Don't just read about derivatives. Walk the desk. Price the tree. Get knocked out once, it's educational.
Our booth is live right now. Scan, sign up, and make your first quote before the next lecture.
Educational simulator, not investment advice. Options involve risk, premiums can go to zero. Data: HKEX contract specifications. Built by our group, FIN 7870.

items.title: Risk
items.desc: options involve risk, premiums can go to zero

text: Central Trader. See you on the desk.
